title: Audit Spine event (v2 — §4.5)
description: One line of an append-only, hash-chained per-loop ledger at .orchestrator/audit/<loop_id>.jsonl (plan §4.5). MATCHES the event emitted by engine-kit/audit/audit_log.py make_event(): fields {loop_id, seq, ts, type, payload, prev_hash, hash}, in that body order. HASH CHAIN: hash = sha256( prev_hash + canonical_json(event_without_hash) ).hexdigest(), where canonical_json = json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':')); the first event's prev_hash is 64 zeros (GENESIS_PREV_HASH); seq starts at 0 and increases by 1. payload is a generic dict (the ledger stores whatever it is given); a per-spawn event carries the execution-context payload in $defs/spawn_payload (G3). This schema validates event STRUCTURE; chain integrity is verified by audit_log.verify_chain. Audit is runtime-owned (Constitution §1.4).

loop_id.description: Threads charter→brief→checkpoint→spawn→trace→verdict→close (G6). The ledger file is named <loop_id>.jsonl.

seq.description: Monotonic per-loop sequence; first event seq=0, each subsequent +1 (verify_events enforces this).

ts.description: Event timestamp, injected by the caller (the pure hash path never reads the clock). Convention: ISO 8601. Stored verbatim as part of the hashed body.

type.description: Event type, e.g. loop_init | charter_loaded | checkpoint | spawn | verdict | close. Free-form string (the ledger is a generic event log).

payload.description: Event payload. Generic — the ledger stores whatever dict it is given. For a per-spawn event this is the execution-context record ($defs/spawn_payload, plan §4.5 G3).

prev_hash.description: sha256 hex (64 lowercase hex chars) of the previous event's hash; 64 zeros for the genesis event (GENESIS_PREV_HASH).

hash.description: sha256 hex over (prev_hash + canonical_json(event_without_hash)). Tampering any byte breaks the chain from this seq on.

spawn_payload.description: Per-spawn execution-context payload (plan §4.5 G3; audit_log.make_spawn_payload). Extends the orchestrator's calls/ record so a loop can be reconstructed with full harness/model/skill/memory/cost context.
